summary: Large warning-cleanup/header-refactor pass centered on `stage.c`: replaced the project-local `dolphin/types.h` shim with direct `Runtime/platform.h` includes, moved stage data types out of `stage.h` into new `src/melee/gr/types.h`, promoted the old `assert_line` macro into baselib as `HSD_ASSERT`, added/typed stage and ground prototypes, and removed very old `baselib_unknown_001.c`/`baselib_unknown_002.c` source files. The PR mattered because implicit function declarations were being eliminated by giving call sites real prototypes and by untangling broad legacy typedef/header placeholders.
what_changed: Deleted `include/dolphin/types.h`, which had only wrapped `Runtime/platform.h` and carried temporary macros such as `assert_line`, `STRUCT_PLACEHOLDER`, `CALLBACK_PLACEHOLDER`, and `UNK_FUNC`.
Replaced many `#include <dolphin/types.h>` uses across Dolphin, baselib, MSL/runtime, and game headers with `#include <Runtime/platform.h>`.

Moved the assert macro into `include/sysdolphin/baselib/debug.h` as `HSD_ASSERT(line, cond)`, preserving the `MUST_MATCH` behavior of using the passed line number versus `__LINE__` outside matching builds.
Updated `.clang-format` `WhitespaceSensitiveMacros` from `assert_line` to `HSD_ASSERT` so assertion macro formatting remains stable.
Converted many baselib/jobjs and other call sites from `assert_line(...)` to `HSD_ASSERT(...)`.
Replaced placeholder macros with explicit forward typedefs, for example `OSThread_Unk1` through `OSThread_Unk4` and `cobj_Unk1` / `cobj_UnkCallback1`.
Created `src/melee/gr/types.h` and moved `InternalStageID`, `StageCameraInfo`, `StageBlastZone`, `StageInfo`, `StageCallbacks`, `StageData`, `StructPairWithStageID`, and `Map` out of `stage.h`.
Shrank `src/melee/gr/stage.h` to mostly includes and function declarations, now depending on `melee/gr/types.h` instead of carrying all stage struct definitions inline.

Tightened `stage.c` prototypes: many zero-argument functions now use `(void)`, stage index parameters became `InternalStageID`, and `Stage_802251B4` was corrected to return `void` rather than returning the result of `func_801C06B8`.
Added `ground.h` prototypes for several `func_801C...` helpers using `StructPairWithStageID*` and for `func_801C06B8(InternalStageID arg0)`.

Changed `func_801C06B8` from `void func_801C06B8(enum_t arg0)` to `void func_801C06B8(InternalStageID arg0)` and used named stage enum cases `IZUMI` and `PSTADIUM` instead of numeric cases `12` and `16`.
Removed old `src/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_001.c` and `src/sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002.c` files, described by the author as early learning-era artifacts.
smart_moves: Removed the broad `dolphin/types.h` catch-all instead of continuing to hide missing dependencies behind it; this forced headers to depend directly on `Runtime/platform.h` and exposed real type/prototype needs.
Moved large stage structs/enums into a dedicated `melee/gr/types.h`, reducing `stage.h` coupling while still allowing `ground.h` and other users to reference shared stage types.
Renamed the temporary `assert_line` compatibility macro to the domain-appropriate `HSD_ASSERT` and placed it in baselib debug infrastructure, matching the existing `__assert`/`HSD_Panic` home.
Kept `MUST_MATCH` line-number semantics inside `HSD_ASSERT`, so the cleanup did not lose matching-sensitive assert behavior.
Replaced macro-generated placeholder declarations with explicit typedef declarations, making unknown structs/callbacks searchable and easier to refine later.
Resolved an uncertain return-type question by aligning `func_801C06B8` and `Stage_802251B4` as `void`, avoiding a misleading `StageData*` return based only on partial matching attempts.
decomp_lessons: Implicit-function-declaration cleanup is often a header architecture task, not just a matter of sprinkling prototypes: shared structs should be factored into smaller type headers that can be included without circular dependencies.
Avoid project-wide shim headers that mix primitive typedefs with unrelated decomp convenience macros; they hide ownership and make later warning passes harder.
When replacing old-style C declarations, use explicit `(void)` on true no-argument functions to prevent old K&R-style function declarations from masking call-site mistakes.
For unknown placeholder types, prefer explicit `typedef struct _Name Name;` and explicit callback typedefs over generator macros; explicit names are easier to grep and evolve.
Assert macros used for matching need formatting support and `MUST_MATCH` semantics preserved when renamed or moved.
If an attempted C match suggests a return value but review evidence indicates callers ignore it and related wrappers are `void`, do not encode a speculative return type into the public prototype.
naming_conventions: Stage IDs continue to use existing enum constants such as `IZUMI` and `PSTADIUM`; the enum still carries a TODO to finish values and use PascalCase.
Unknown forward structs use `_Module_UnkN` tags and `Module_UnkN` typedef names, for example `_OSThread_Unk1` / `OSThread_Unk1`.
Unknown callback typedefs are named with an `UnkCallbackN` suffix, for example `cobj_UnkCallback1`.
Shared ground/stage types are grouped under `melee/gr/types.h` rather than being embedded in `stage.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The review excerpt shows an attempted `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` inline assembly version of `func_801C06B8` with `asm StageData* func_801C06B8(InternalStageID arg0)`, `nofralloc`, raw PowerPC instructions, and `clang-format off`, but the merged diff kept a C implementation returning `void`.

The final `func_801C06B8` C form uses `InternalStageID` and named enum cases, while preserving the control flow that calls `func_800178E8`, `func_801CD2D4` for `IZUMI`, and `func_801D511C` for `PSTADIUM`.
For matching-sensitive asserts, `HSD_ASSERT(line, cond)` maps to `__assert(__FILE__, line, #cond)` under `MUST_MATCH` and to `__assert(__FILE__, __LINE__, #cond)` otherwise.
Stage wrapper `Stage_802251B4` was changed from returning `s32` and returning `func_801C06B8(...)` to `void` and simply calling `func_801C06B8(...)`, matching the reviewer’s return-type feedback.
review_feedback: ribbanya noted on `src/melee/gr/ground.c` that they tried to make `func_801C06B8` match but got bored and invited others to try.
r-burns suggested it was more likely that `func_801C06B8` returns `void`, as does `Stage_802251B4`.
A later suggestion removed the TODO/comment path that claimed `func_801C06B8` needed to return `StageData*`; the final diff uses a `void` return.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining includes of `dolphin/types.h` after PR 664.
Find remaining uses of `assert_line` after migration to `HSD_ASSERT`.
Audit `InternalStageID` enum TODO for PascalCase naming and incomplete values.
Search for remaining old-style zero-argument declarations missing `(void)`.
Review users of `Stage_802251B4` and `func_801C06B8` to confirm `void` return assumptions.
Investigate whether old `baselib_unknown_001.c` and `baselib_unknown_002.c` contents were truly dead or migrated elsewhere.
